architecture_style: Art Deco suspension bridge (Strauss chief engineer)
description: International Orange span linking San Francisco to Marin Headlands. Fort Point and Battery Spencer offer classic viewpoints.
facts: Sidewalk hours flip between east and west sides by season.
Fog can erase the towers within minutes—check marine layer forecasts.
history: Depression-era project with Art Deco towers; retrofit added seismic isolation after Loma Prieta lessons.
significance: The city's most reproduced engineering landmark.

description: Moving National Historic Landmark climbing Nob Hill ridges; gripmen operate manual brakes on steep grades.
facts: Clipper card and visitor passports reduce queueing at busy stops.
Cable machinery at Washington–Mason barn can be toured.
history: Andrew Smith Hallidene's rope-drive system survived earthquakes and bus competition as a heritage fleet.
significance: Working museum of Victorian urban traction.

description: Audio tours through cellblocks narrated by former guards and inmates; seabird nesting closes some paths seasonally.
facts: Only concessionaire boats may dock; tickets sell out weeks ahead in summer.
Night tours add lighthouse and hospital wing access.
history: Military prison preceded gangster-era federal pen; Native occupation in 1969–1971 reframed its symbolism.
significance: Bay islands park with layered carceral history.

description: Pastel Victorian fronts facing downtown skyline from Alamo Square lawn—postcard framing of old and new skylines.
facts: Park slope gets muddy after rain; bring a blanket for picnics.
TV opening credits made this view globally familiar.
history: Post-1906 paint campaigns emphasised polychrome trim now maintained by homeowners' associations.
significance: Neighbourhood preservation showcase beside Western Addition parks.

address: Russian Hill between Hyde and Leavenworth
architecture_style: Residential brick road with hydrangea planters
description: One-way hairpin garden street designed to tame Russian Hill grades; tourist cars queue on summer weekends.
facts: Pedestrians can descend stairs on both sides for photos without driving.
Cable car Powell–Hyde line passes the top of the block.
history: Resident Carl Henry proposed curves to reduce 27% grades that early autos could not climb.
significance: Controlled traffic-calming experiment turned pop-culture set piece.

description: Ferry Building Marketplace — landmark in San Francisco.
facts: Ferries still depart to Oakland and Larkspur from slips behind the hall.
Clock tower tours are occasional—check schedules.
history: Survived the 1906 quake as a waterfront landmark; freeway era hid it until Embarcadero removal.
significance: Food destination anchor on the eastern waterfront.

description: Swan pond reflecting a faux-ruin colonnade originally built for the Panama-Pacific Expo.
facts: Wedding photography crowds peak Saturday afternoons.
Exploratorium moved out but theatre remains active.
history: Bernard Maybeck's temporary palace was reconstructed in concrete for permanence.
significance: Romantic backdrop between Marina flats and the Presidio.

description: Depression-era murals line the lobby before elevator rides to bay-view balconies.
facts: Filbert Steps approach rewards walkers climbing from the Wharf.
Parking on the summit circle is extremely limited.
history: Lillie Hitchcock Coit's bequest funded firefighter tribute symbolism.
significance: 360° orientation spike visible from many hill neighbourhoods.

description: California sea lions haul out on floating docks beside carousel and sourdough chowder stalls.
facts: Winter counts peak when herring spawn nearby.
Bay cruises depart from adjacent slips.
history: Animals chose the docks voluntarily after the 1989 earthquake marina shifts.
significance: Free wildlife spectacle amid tourist retail density.

description: Stone arch marking entry to the oldest North American Chinatown's steep Grant Avenue shops.
facts: Portsmouth Square sits two blocks north for tai chi mornings.
Stockton Street markets are less touristy than Grant.
history: Post-earthquake rebuilds layered neon signage onto Victorian brick.
significance: Symbolic threshold between Financial District and community lanes.

description: Legion of Honour — highest French order of merit.
facts: Free first Tuesdays draw long morning lines.
Same-day ticket discounts link to de Young in Golden Gate Park.
history: Adolph Spreckels' memorial museum echoes Paris's Legion façade on Pacific cliffs.
significance: Fine arts complement to science across the bridge approaches.

description: Americas, Oceania, and costume collections with an observation level over tree canopy.
facts: Friday night events mix DJs with gallery access.
Japanese Tea Garden sits beside the east entrance.
history: 1900s expo structure failed in earthquakes; new building twists to resist lateral loads.
significance: Park-side counterpart to the Legion across the dunes.

description: Exploratorium — museum in San Francisco.
facts: Check opening hours and ticketing on official sites before travel.
Crowds peak on weekends and public holidays.
